evals.name: narrative-skill-pdf-style
evals.prompt: ok i need a skill for when i'm doing literature reviews and converting research papers to a clean markdown summary. like, the format should always be: TLDR (2 sentences max), key contributions (3-5 bullets), methodology (1 paragraph), limitations (1 paragraph), and a 'why this matters for my research' section at the end. i mostly read AI alignment papers but also some neuro stuff

expectations.text: Skill is classified as narrative (no eval loop, no multi-stage handoff)
SKILL.md has both name and description in frontmatter
META.md is created separately from SKILL.md
Description includes both what (paper-to-markdown summary) and when (literature review context)
No pipeline ceremony (workflow.json, schemas/, stages/) is created for this narrative skill

evals.name: pipeline-skill-data-validation
evals.prompt: i'm building a thing that takes csv files from our finance team and validates them against a schema, then produces a report of which rows fail validation and why. need to be able to test it on a bunch of csvs since the team's csvs are inconsistent. should be runnable in our CI

expectations.text: Skill is classified as pipeline (has structured input → output → verification)
schemas/ directory created with at least one .schema.json
stages/ directory has at least one stage script with the documented CLI shape (--in, --out)
evals/evals.json includes test prompts that exercise the validation step

evals.name: drift-detection-existing-skill
evals.prompt: we have an existing skill in /tmp/our-old-skill that we wrote like 6 months ago and the grading.json files coming out of it have fields like 'name' and 'met' instead of 'text' and 'passed'. need to migrate it to the skill-creator-strict conventions

expectations.text: scripts.validate is run against the existing grading.json files
The validator's structured error message is shown to the user
Migration is performed by editing the producing stage, not by weakening the schema
Post-migration, scripts.validate passes against the same files
